name: x-filter
description: Score and filter topics for X content creation using weighted criteria. Use when user wants to evaluate collected materials, filter topics by score, or mentions "filter topics", "score materials", "x-filter", "选题筛选". Applies 10-point scoring system with customizable weights.
capabilities: Score topics on 4 weighted criteria: trending (0-4), controversy (0-2), value (0-3), relevance (0-1)
Filter materials into 3 tiers: creation pool (7+ points), pending (5-6 points), rejected (<5 points)
Read user profile for personalized relevance scoring based on account positioning
Generate structured markdown reports with topic recommendations and next steps
Support input from x-collect output, manual topic lists, or raw text evaluation
Allow customizable scoring weights and threshold in user-profile.yaml
author: kangarooking
securitySummary: All static findings are false positives. The skill is a content scoring tool for X (Twitter) content creation. The scanner misidentified markdown documentation syntax (code blocks, YAML examples) as shell backtick execution. No actual command execution, file system threats, or cryptographic operations exist in this skill.
valueStatement: Content creators spend too much time deciding which topics to write about. This skill scores and filters collected materials using a 10-point weighted system based on trending, controversy, value, and relevance criteria, helping prioritize high-potential content.
limitations: Does not collect topics independently; requires input from x-collect or user-provided list
Scoring is subjective and based on human judgment of criteria
Does not create content; only filters and recommends topics for creation
Default domains assume AI, tech, entrepreneurship focus if no user profile exists

useCases.targetUser: Content creators who maintain active X accounts and need daily topic selection
useCases.title: Daily Content Planning
useCases.description: Process collected素材 (materials) from x-collect to identify the best topics for today is X content. Get a ranked list with scores and creation suggestions.

useCases.targetUser: Writers planning long-form thread content who want data-driven topic selection
useCases.title: Thread Topic Evaluation
useCases.description: Evaluate a list of potential thread topics against trending, controversy, value, and relevance criteria. Receive scored recommendations with priority rankings.

useCases.targetUser: Content managers organizing large topic backlogs who need systematic prioritization
useCases.title: Content Audit and Cleanup
useCases.description: Review pending content ideas by scoring each against defined criteria. Move high-scoring topics to the creation pool and archive low-priority items.

promptTemplates.title: Quick Topic Scoring
promptTemplates.scenario: Scoring a small list of 3-5 topics with default criteria

promptTemplates.title: Batch Material Processing
promptTemplates.scenario: Processing full output from the x-collect skill

promptTemplates.title: Custom Weight Scoring
promptTemplates.scenario: Using custom scoring weights for specialized content strategy

promptTemplates.title: Domain-Specific Evaluation
promptTemplates.scenario: Getting personalized scoring based on specific account positioning

bestPractices: Use with x-collect first to gather materials, then x-filter to score them, and x-create to produce content for a complete workflow
Configure user-profile.md with your account domains and persona style for accurate relevance scoring
Set threshold to 7 by default; lower it to 6 if you need more topics or raise to 8 for strict curation
antiPatterns: Do not use x-filter alone without x-collect or manual topic input; it does not collect topics itself
Do not expect the skill to create content; it only filters and recommends topics for creation
Do not skip the user profile setup if you want personalized relevance scoring based on your account focus

faq.question: What input formats does x-filter accept?
faq.answer: x-filter accepts three input types: output from x-collect, manual topic lists you provide, and raw text for evaluation. The skill processes each material and scores it against the four criteria.

faq.question: How does the scoring system work?
faq.answer: The skill uses a 10-point system with four criteria: Trending (0-4 points), Controversy (0-2 points), Value (0-3 points), and Relevance (0-1 point). Topics scoring 7+ enter the creation pool.

faq.question: Can I customize the scoring weights?
faq.answer: Yes. You can set custom weights for each criterion in user-profile.yaml. The default weights are trending=4, controversy=2, value=3, relevance=1. You can also adjust the 7-point threshold.

faq.question: What is the user profile file for?
faq.answer: The user profile at ~/.claude/skills/x-create/references/user-profile.md defines your account domains and persona style. This enables personalized relevance scoring based on how well each topic aligns with your content focus.

faq.question: Does x-filter create content?
faq.answer: No. x-filter only scores and filters topics. After filtering, you can use x-create to generate actual X content from the recommended topics. These skills work together in a complete workflow.

faq.question: What happens if I do not have a user profile?
faq.answer: Without a user profile, the skill assumes default domains of AI/科技, 创业, and 个人成长 for relevance scoring. Setting up a user profile improves scoring accuracy for your specific content focus.
